overview: Cleans strings by normalizing quotes, punctuation, and whitespace ready for further processing. By default, **all whitespace is normalized before any further processing** — all multiple space, tab, etc. characters are replaced with a single instance. Operates on filter events and is typically used in TMX processing pipelines.

normalizeQuotes.help:
Replaces all typographic/curly quotation marks with straight double quotes (`"`) and all typographic apostrophes with single straight quotes (`'`).

Useful for normalizing text from sources that use locale-specific quote characters (e.g., “” ‘’ «») into a consistent ASCII representation.

checkCharacters.help:
Detects and **removes entire text units** that contain common corrupt character strings.

This catches encoding artifacts and garbage characters that indicate data corruption, preventing them from propagating into translation memories or downstream processing.

matchRegexExpressions.help: Marks segments matching built-in regular expressions for removal.
notes: This option is **not currently used** — it exists as a placeholder for future built-in patterns.

matchUserRegex.help:
Removes text units whose content matches a user-defined regular expression.

Use this to filter out segments that match known patterns you want to exclude (e.g., code-only strings, placeholder text).

pruneTextUnit.help:
Removes text units that have been **marked for removal** by other checks in this step, or that have **no target text**.

This is the final cleanup pass — without it, segments flagged by character checks or regex matching remain in the output.
notes: Depends on the results of other enabled checks (`checkCharacters`, `matchRegexExpressions`, `matchUserRegex`). Disabling this effectively prevents those checks from removing any content.

normalizePunctuations.help:
Standardizes spacing around punctuation marks to follow **English writing conventions** — e.g., no space before a period or comma, one space after.

This may produce incorrect results for languages like French that require spaces before certain punctuation (e.g., `;`, `?`, `!`).
notes: Only appropriate for English-style punctuation rules. Do not enable for French, or other languages with different punctuation spacing conventions.

keepCrNlIntact.help:
Preserves carriage return (CR) and newline (NL) characters during whitespace normalization.

By default, the step normalizes **all** whitespace (spaces, tabs, CR, NL) into single ASCII spaces. Enable this to keep line breaks intact while still normalizing spaces and tabs.
notes: Only affects the whitespace normalization pass that runs before all other processing.

limitations: Does not work with **bi-directional languages** (Arabic, Hebrew, etc.).
The `matchRegexExpressions` option (built-in regex patterns) is not currently implemented.
processingNotes: Whitespace normalization runs **first**, before any other processing — all multiple spaces, tabs, and (unless `keepCrNlIntact` is enabled) CR/NL characters are replaced with a single ASCII space.
Checks that mark segments for removal (`checkCharacters`, `matchUserRegex`) only take effect if `pruneTextUnit` is enabled to perform the actual removal.
